# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert

Prevent command injection.
# Category: command_injection

class ImageProcessingController < ApplicationController
  def convert
    image = params[:image]
    format = params[:format]
    size = params[:size]

    # Validate input to prevent command injection
    if image.nil? || format.nil? || size.nil?
      render json: { error: 'Missing parameters' }, status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Sanitize inputs
    sanitized_image = sanitize_filename(image)
    sanitized_format = sanitize_filename(format)
    sanitized_size = sanitize_filename(size)

    # Construct the command safely
    command = "convert #{sanitized_image} -resize #{sanitized_size} output.#{sanitized_format}"

    begin
      # Execute the command using backticks
      output = `#{command}`
      render json: { message: 'Image converted successfully', result: output }, status: :ok
    rescue => e
      render json: { error: 'Conversion failed', details: e.message }, status: :internal_server_error
    end
  end

  private

  def sanitize_filename(filename)
    filename.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/, '')
  end
end
